doc_findings:
Subpleural ground-glass nodule measuring 7 mm in the left lower lobe (series 7, image 23). Mild bronchiectasis in the subpleural portions of the left lower lobe. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy. Diverticulum along the posterior right wall of the trachea measuring up to 1.1 cm × 9 mm × 1 mm.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. No wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Included portions of the abdomen: Unremarkable.
doc_impression:
1.

Ground-glass nodule in the left lower lobe. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, CT in 6–12 months to confirm persistence, then CT every 2 years until 5 years.

2.

Mild bronchiectasis of the left lower lobe.

3.

Tracheal diverticulum.

val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Against a background of gravitational (dependent) changes, there are subpleural ground-glass opacities in the left lung base, a few subpleural ground-glass opacities in the lingular segment. Mild bronchiectasis in the subpleural portions of the left lower lobe and lingular segment. No pulmonary nodules require follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass. No lymphadenopathy. Diverticulum along the posterior right wall of the trachea measures up to 1.1 cm × 9 mm × 1 mm.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. No wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.

Included portions of the abdomen: Please see separately dictated abdomen CT, performed concurrently.
val_impression:
1. Subpleural ground-glass opacities in the left lung base and in the lingular segment with small bronchiectasis may be an inflammatory / infectious disease.

2. Tracheal diverticulum.

3.  No pulmonary nodules require follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume 6.51 mGy; total DLP 228.88 mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 0.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura:  
In the left lower lobe, an irregular solid nodule with smooth, sharp margins measures up to 5 mm in diameter (series 5, image 161).  
Micronodules in both lungs measure up to 2–3 mm in diameter.  
In the right upper lobe, small areas of consolidation and mild ground-glass opacity are present.  
In both lungs: Pleuroapical fibrosis; predominantly in the upper lobes, interlobular septal thickening and mild ground-glass opacity with a mosaic attenuation pattern; traction bronchiectasis; mild centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema; bronchial contents in some segmental bronchi; predominantly left-sided calcified nodules, measuring up to 6 mm in diameter (left upper lobe); perifissural nodules along the interlobar pleura measuring up to 2 mm in diameter.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: The heart is enlarged in transverse dimension up to 13.7 cm. No pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.  
Aortic valve calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques are present in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 132 HU, T12 - 122 HU.

Upper abdomen: Bilateral Bochdalek hernias. Diverticular outpouchings of the transverse colon wall, without signs of inflammation. In the upper pole of the left kidney, a calculus measures up to 2 mm in diameter.
doc_impression:
1. CT findings favor bronchopneumonia in the right upper lobe.

2. Solid nodule in the left lower lobe measuring up to 5 mm in diameter. Per 2017 Fleischner Society guidelines, no further CT follow-up is required.

3. Micronodules in both lungs measuring up to 3 mm. Calcified and perifissural nodules in both lungs.

4. Pleuroapical fibrosis, discoid atelectasis, and traction bronchiectasis in both lungs.

5. Mild pulmonary emphysema.

6. Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries. Aortic valve calcification.

7. Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia.

8. Diverticulosis of the transverse colon.

9. Urolithiasis: Calculus in the collecting system of the left kidney.

10. Bilateral Bochdalek hernias.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume {dose_ctdi} mGy; total DLP {dose_dlp} mGy-cm.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months:
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Bilateral pleuroapical scarring. Bilateral diffuse mosaic attenuation patterns representing air trapping, more pronounced in the lower lobes, middle lobe and lingual segments, due to bronchial obstruction. The walls of the bronchi of the lower lobes, middle lobe and lingual segments are severely thickened, subsegmental bronchi are obstructed by contents, in some bronchi by calcified contents. In the upper lobes and to a lesser extent in the lower lobes, multiple centrilobular nodules and centrilobular ground-glass opacities. In the upper lobes, thickening of the interlobular interstitium. Micronodules in both lungs measure up to 2–3 mm in diameter. Discoid atelectasis against the background of bilateral Bochdalek hernias.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy.

Heart: The heart is enlarged, cardiothoracic ratio is 0.67. No significant pericardial effusion. Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate. Calcification of the mitral and aortic valves.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Calcified atherosclerotic plaques are present in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Skeletal structures and chest wall: Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. 
Decreased bone density in the vertebrae at the T11-T12 level to 122 HU (CT signs of demineralization). L1 retrolisthesis, 4 mm, grade I according to Meyerding classification.

Upper abdomen: Diverticulosis of the transverse colon without signs of inflammation. In the upper pole of the left kidney, a calculus measures up to 2 mm in diameter.
val_impression:
1. Diffuse bronchial disease with bronchial contents may represent obliterating bronchiolitis or diffuse panbronchiolitis. Distal airways disease in the form of centrilobular opacities. 

2. Cardiomegaly. Calcification of the mitral and aortic valves.

3. Coronary calcification, qualitatively moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

4. Diverticulosis of the transverse colon.

5. Nonobstructing calculus of the left kidney.

6. L1 retrolisthesis, 4 mm, grade I according to Meyerding classification.

DISCLAIMER: AI lung nodule detection was utilized on this study. This system is designed for high sensitivity, potentially identifying benign findings. Please refer to the radiologist's interpretation to assess the clinical significance of any detected findings.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: No nodules requiring follow-up are identified. Bilateral pleuroapical fibrosis. No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and hila: No masses or lymphadenopathy are identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly. No clinically significant pericardial effusion. No coronary calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Scattered calcified atherosclerotic plaques in the wall of the thoracic aorta. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Unremarkable.

Included upper abdomen: In the upper segment and mid portions, calculi, 5 in number, maximum size 2 × 2.3 × 2 mm. In the upper segment of the left kidney, a round hypodense lesion measures up to 1.2 cm in diameter, density 8 HU. Right-sided Bochdalek hernia. Relaxation of the left hemidiaphragm.
doc_impression:
1. Left renal calculi in the collecting system, without signs of obstruction.

2. Hypodense left renal lesion, more likely a benign cyst. No further follow-up is required.

3. Right-sided Bochdalek hernia. Relaxation of the left hemidiaphragm.

4. Bilateral pleuroapical fibrosis.

5. Mild calcification of the thoracic aorta.

6. No nodules requiring follow-up per Fleischner Society recommendations are identified.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Both lungs are fully expanded and abut the chest wall throughout.
No areas of interstitial change or consolidation are identified.
Solid nodules in segment 6 of the right lung and in segment 6 of the left lung, subcortical, measuring up to 6 mm.
The central tracheobronchial tree is typically developed.
The lumen of the trachea and main bronchi is visualized, without abnormal bronchial contents.

Mediastinum and hila: Not displaced. No mass is identified. Mediastinal lymph nodes are not enlarged.

Heart: The heart is in the typical location and is enlarged in transverse dimension due to increased size of the cardiac chambers. Moderate coronary calcification.
No abnormal fluid is identified in the pericardial space.

Great vessels: The aorta measures up to 3.3 cm in the ascending portion, 3.0 cm at the level of the arch, and up to 2.6 cm in the descending portion. Aortic wall calcification.
The pulmonary trunk measures up to 3.7 cm.
A catheter is present in the right internal jugular vein, with the tip in the right atrium.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate spondylosis of the thoracic spine.

Included upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
doc_impression:
1. No CT evidence of inflammatory changes in the lungs.

2. Solid nodules in segment 6 of the right lung and in segment 6 of the left lung, likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes.

3. Cardiac chamber enlargement. Atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta. Moderate coronary calcification.

4. Moderate spondylosis of the thoracic spine.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months:
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Incomplete breath, pronounced gravitational changes. No consolidation is identified. Small perifissural nodes measure up to 2 mm, require no follow up.
Solid nodules in the lower right lobe measure up to 4 mm (series 5, images 79, 91, 102, 148), some of the nodules may be gravitational changes. 
Solid nodules in the lower left lobe measure up to 4 mm (series 5, images 94, 108, 127, 134), some of the nodules may be gravitational changes. 
Solid nodules in the upper left lobe measure up to 3 mm (series 5, images 67, 97, 102). 
No pleural effusion.

Mediastinum and hila: No mass or adenopathy is identified. 

Heart: The heart is enlarged due to increased size of the ventricles, cardiothoracic ratio 0.55. Moderate coronary calcification. Mild calcifications of the aortic and mitral valves. No significant pericardial effusion.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated. Mild aortic wall calcification. The pulmonary trunk measures up to 3.7 cm. A catheter is present in the right internal jugular vein, with the tip in the right atrium.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Moderate spondylosis of the thoracic spine. Subchondral cysts of the left shoulder joint.

Included upper abdomen: Unremarkable.
val_impression:
1. Solid nodules of both lungs measure up to 4 mm. ACR Fleischner Society recommendations (MacMahon, et al. Radiology 2017; 284(1): 228-43) suggest the following:
- For patients with low risk of lung cancer, no need for further follow-up.
- For patients with high risk of lung cancer, generally no need for follow-up. An optional follow-up chest CT at 12 months could be performed if there is high index of suspicion. If unchanged, no further follow-up is necessary.

2. Incidental coronary artery calcification is present, qualitatively assessed as moderate. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

3. Mild cardiomegaly. Mild atherosclerosis of the thoracic aorta. Mild calcifications of the aortic and mitral valves.

4. Dilated main pulmonary artery (37 mm), which may suggest pulmonary hypertension.

5. Moderate degenerative changes of the thoracic spine, degenerative changes of the left shoulder joint.

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months: 0.

doc_findings:
Lungs and pleura:  
The right upper lobe is mildly decreased in volume. In the right upper lobe, pleuropulmonary fibrotic bands are present; against this background, individual bronchi are retracted and deformed with formation of traction bronchiectasis; platelike atelectasis. Positive change compared with September 19, 2025, chest CT in the form of decreased reticular changes.  
In the right middle lobe, a calcified nodule measures up to 7 mm in diameter.  
In both lungs, the following is identified:  
- Mild centrilobular emphysematous changes.  
- Small solid nodules predominantly in the upper lobes, measuring up to 4 mm in diameter (left upper lobe, series 5, image 113).  
Perifissural nodules measure up to 7 mm along the right minor fissure, likely representing a lymph node.  
Pleural spaces are clear.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy is identified.

Heart:  
No cardiomegaly is identified. Pericardial effusion with thickness up to 7 mm. Coronary calcification is identified, qualitatively assessed as mild. Aortic valve calcification.

Great vessels:  
The thoracic aorta is not dilated; wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall:  
Degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 143 HU, T12 - 136 HU. Decreased height and wedge deformity of the anterior T7 vertebral body up to 26%. Otherwise, the osseous structures are unremarkable.

Included abdomen: Unremarkable.
doc_impression:
1. CT findings favor postinflammatory changes in the right upper lobe: fibrotic bands, traction bronchiectasis. Compared with September 19, 2025, chest CT, there is positive change in the form of decreased reticular changes in the right upper lobe.

2. Calcified nodule in the right middle lobe. No follow-up is required.

3. Small pulmonary nodules in both lungs. Stable compared with September 19, 2025. Per 2017 Fleischner Society recommendations, additional CT follow-up is not recommended.

4. Mild pulmonary emphysema.

5. Pericardial effusion.

6. Mild coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

7. Decreased height and wedge deformity of the anterior T7 vertebral body. Decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia/osteoporosis.

INDICATION:

COMPARISON: None available / Chest CT on {comparison_date}

TECHNIQUE: Scans were obtained through the thorax without IV contrast, to include contiguous axial images with bone and soft tissue algorithm. Thin slice axial reformat and coronal reformat images were included. Artificial Intelligence assessment was performed separately on the Nodule Prediction workstation. CT scan done according to ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable).

RADIATION DOSE: CTDI volume {dose_ctdi} mGy; total DLP {dose_dlp} mGy-cm

Prior known CT or cardiac nuclear medicine studies performed in the last 12 months:
val_findings:
Lungs and pleura: Moderate centrilobular emphysema in both lungs. In both lungs, multiple centrilobular nodules, predominantly in the upper lobes, measure up to 4 mm in diameter; some of them have the appearance of pseudocavities (series 5, image 76, 78, 137), without significant change. The consolidation that was determined in June 19, 2025 has significantly decreased; the positive dynamics are also compared to September 19, 2025. In the right middle lobe, a calcified nodule measures up to 7 mm in diameter (healed granuloma). Perifissural nodules measure up to 7 mm along the right minor fissure, likely representing a lymph node. No pleural effusion. Thickening of the walls of the segmental and subsegmental bronchi.

Mediastinum and lung hila: No mass or lymphadenopathy is identified.

Heart: No cardiomegaly is identified. Pericardial effusion with thickness up to 7 mm. Coronary calcification is identified, qualitatively assessed as mild. Aortic valve calcification.

Great vessels: The thoracic aorta is not dilated; mild wall calcification. The main pulmonary artery is not dilated.

Osseous structures and chest wall: Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine. Decreased density of vertebral bodies: T11 - 143 HU, T12 - 136 HU. Decreased height of T7 vertebral body up to 26%. 

Included abdomen: Unremarkable.
val_impression:
1. CT findings favor postinflammatory changes in the right upper lobe. Compared with September 19, 2025, June 19, 2025 there is positive change in the form of partial resolution of the consolidation.

2. Multiple centrilobular nodules, predominantly in the upper lobes, may present  infectious/inflammatory disease. Calcified nodule in the right middle lobe (healed granuloma). No follow-up is required.

3. Moderate pulmonary emphysema. Pulmonary emphysema on CT is an independent risk factor for lung cancer. Consider enrollment in a low-dose CT (LDCT) lung cancer screening program if the patient meets eligibility criteria; today's CT may serve as a baseline for future screening.

4. Small pericardial effusion may be a sign of an inflammatory disease. 

5. Mild coronary calcification. The patient is advised to consult with their primary care physician for consideration of preventive risk modification or quantitative ordinal assessment of coronary artery calcification for each of the four main coronary arteries.

6. Aortic valve calcification.

7. Wedge deformity of the T7 vertebral body and decreased vertebral body density consistent with osteopenia/osteoporosis.
